summary: Validated suggestion review, selection, edit-before-apply, dismiss, additive merge, and keyboard interaction on a live draft pack. The flow produced five suggestion cards, allowed keyboard toggling, preserved edited values on apply, and kept the existing shared axis intact while adding only the selected suggestions.

assertions.reason: A successful suggestion run rendered five suggestion cards, each with the axis title/key plus the description and low/mid/high anchors visible in the live page.
The cards were independently selectable: all started checked, and keyboard Space toggled the first suggestion off while the remaining suggestions stayed selected.
Inline edit mode exposed editable fields for a suggestion, and the edited label/description were preserved when the selected suggestion was applied into the pack.
Applying the selected suggestions merged them into sharedAxes without overwriting the pre-existing existing_support_style axis; the pack moved from one existing axis to three total axes after apply.
Dismissing the suggestion panel left the pack unchanged: after dismiss the body text still showed only the original existing_support_style axis and no added suggestions.
Suggestion generation worked on a pack that already had an existing shared axis, and applying selected suggestions added new axes instead of replacing the original one.
Keyboard navigation reached the suggestion checkbox from the action buttons, and pressing Space toggled selection without requiring the mouse.

frictions: The suggestion labels vary between LLM runs, so evidence had to be anchored to the observed live labels rather than fixed expected keys.
The page is long enough that body-text captures were more reliable than a single screenshot for confirming the full post-apply axis list.
